prompt:
[{'role': 'system', 'content': 'You are an expert SQL assistant. Given a database schema and a natural language question, write a correct SQL query that answers the question.
Return ONLY the SQL query inside a ```sql ... ``` code block.'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': "### Question
What is the event that has the highest attendance of the students from the Student_Club?
### Schema
{'event': ['event_id', 'event_name', 'event_date', 'type', 'notes', 'location', 'status'], 'major': ['major_id', 'major_name', 'department', 'college'], 'zip_code': ['zip_code', 'type', 'city', 'county', 'state', 'short_state'], 'attendance': ['link_to_event', 'link_to_member'], 'budget': ['budget_id', 'category', 'spent', 'remaining', 'amount', 'event_status', 'link_to_event'], 'expense': ['expense_id', 'expense_description', 'expense_date', 'cost', 'approved', 'link_to_member', 'link_to_budget'], 'income': ['income_id', 'date_received', 'amount', 'source', 'notes', 'link_to_member'], 'member': ['member_id', 'first_name', 'last_name', 'email', 'position', 't_shirt_size', 'phone', 'zip', 'link_to_major']}"}]
solution: SELECT T1.event_name FROM event AS T1 INNER JOIN attendance AS T2 ON T1.event_id = T2.link_to_event GROUP BY T1.event_name ORDER BY COUNT(T2.link_to_event) DESC LIMIT 1
completion:
```sql
SELECT e.event_name
FROM event e
JOIN (
    SELECT event_id, MAX(attendance) as max_attendance
    FROM (
        SELECT e.event_id, COUNT(a.link_to_member) as attendance
        FROM attendance a
        JOIN event e ON a.link_to_event = e.event_id
        JOIN member m ON a.link_to_member = m.member_id
        JOIN zip_code zc ON m.zip = zc.zip_code
        WHERE m.link_to_major IN (
            SELECT major_id
            FROM member m
            JOIN zip_code zc ON m.zip = zc.zip_code
            WHERE zc.type = 'Student_Club'
        )
        GROUP BY e.event_id
    ) t
    GROUP BY event_id
) m ON e.event_id = m.event_id
WHERE m.max_attendance = t.attendance
ORDER BY e.event_name DESC
LIMIT 1;
```

Return ONLY the SQL query inside a ```sql ... ``` code block.'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': "### Question
Among the events attended by more than 10 members of the Student_Club, how many of them are meetings?
### Schema
{'event': ['event_id', 'event_name', 'event_date', 'type', 'notes', 'location', 'status'], 'major': ['major_id', 'major_name', 'department', 'college'], 'zip_code': ['zip_code', 'type', 'city', 'county', 'state', 'short_state'], 'attendance': ['link_to_event', 'link_to_member'], 'budget': ['budget_id', 'category', 'spent', 'remaining', 'amount', 'event_status', 'link_to_event'], 'expense': ['expense_id', 'expense_description', 'expense_date', 'cost', 'approved', 'link_to_member', 'link_to_budget'], 'income': ['income_id', 'date_received', 'amount', 'source', 'notes', 'link_to_member'], 'member': ['member_id', 'first_name', 'last_name', 'email', 'position', 't_shirt_size', 'phone', 'zip', 'link_to_major']}"}]
solution: SELECT T1.event_name FROM event AS T1  INNER JOIN attendance AS T2 ON T1.event_id = T2.link_to_event GROUP BY T1.event_id  HAVING COUNT(T2.link_to_event) > 10 EXCEPT SELECT T1.event_name  FROM event AS T1  WHERE T1.type = 'Meeting'
completion:
```sql
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT a.link_to_event) AS meeting_count
FROM attendance a
JOIN attendance m ON a.link_to_event = m.link_to_event
WHERE a.link_to_event IN (
    SELECT e.link_to_event
    FROM attendance e
    GROUP BY e.link_to_event
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT e.link_to_member) > 10
)
AND m.link_to_event IN (
    SELECT e.link_to_event
    FROM attendance e
    WHERE e.link_to_member IN (
        SELECT l.link_to_member
        FROM attendance l
        GROUP BY l.link_to_member
        HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT l.link_to_event) > 10
    )
)
AND a.link_to_event = m.link_to_event;
```

### Schema
{'event': ['event_id', 'event_name', 'event_date', 'type', 'notes', 'location', 'status'], 'major': ['major_id', 'major_name', 'department', 'college'], 'zip_code': ['zip_code', 'type', 'city', 'county', 'state', 'short_state'], 'attendance': ['link_to_event', 'link_to_member'], 'budget': ['budget_id', 'category', 'spent', 'remaining', 'amount', 'event_status', 'link_to_event'], 'expense': ['expense_id', 'expense_description', 'expense_date', 'cost', 'approved', 'link_to_member', 'link_to_budget'], 'income': ['income_id', 'date_received', 'amount', 'source', 'notes', 'link_to_member'], 'member': ['member_id', 'first_name', 'last_name', 'email', 'position', 't_shirt_size', 'phone', 'zip', 'link_to_major']}"}]
solution: SELECT T1.event_name FROM event AS T1 INNER JOIN attendance AS T2 ON T1.event_id = T2.link_to_event GROUP BY T1.event_id HAVING COUNT(T2.link_to_event) > 20 EXCEPT SELECT T1.event_name FROM event AS T1  WHERE T1.type = 'Fundraiser'
completion:
```sql
SELECT e.event_name
FROM event e
JOIN attendance a ON e.event_id = a.link_to_event
JOIN budget b ON e.event_id = b.link_to_event
JOIN expense ex ON b.event_status = ex.link_to_budget
JOIN income inc ON b.event_status = inc.link_to_income
WHERE e.attendance > 20 AND e.type != 'fundraiser' AND e.type NOT IN ('fundraiser')
```

solution: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T2.event_name = 'Yearly Kickoff' THEN T1.amount ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / SUM(CASE WHEN T2.event_name = 'October Meeting' THEN T1.amount ELSE 0 END) FROM budget AS T1 INNER JOIN event AS T2 ON T1.link_to_event = T2.event_id WHERE T1.category = 'Advertisement' AND T2.type = 'Meeting'
completion:

```sql
    (CASE 
        WHEN b.category = 'Advertisement' THEN COUNT(*)
    END) AS ad_times,
    (CASE 
        WHEN b.category = 'Speaker' THEN COUNT(*)
    END) AS speaker_times

    budget b
```

completion:

```sql
            WHEN T2.category = 'Yearly Kickoff' THEN T1.spent 
            ELSE 0 
        END) / SUM(T1.spent)) * 100 AS Percentage

JOIN 
    (
        SELECT DISTINCT category 
        FROM budget 
        WHERE category = 'Yearly Kickoff'
    ) AS T2
ON 
    T1.category = T2.category;
```

solution: SELECT T2.event_name FROM attendance AS T1 INNER JOIN event AS T2 ON T2.event_id = T1.link_to_event INNER JOIN member AS T3 ON T1.link_to_member = T3.member_id WHERE T3.position = 'Vice President' AND T2.location = '900 E. Washington St.' AND T2.type = 'Social'

prompt:
[{'role': 'system', 'content': 'You are an expert SQL assistant. Given a database schema and a natural language question, write a correct SQL query that answers the question.
Return ONLY the SQL query inside a ```sql ... ``` code block.'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': "### Question
Among the members with t-shirt size of medium, what is the percentage of the amount 50 received by the Student_Club?
### Schema
{'event': ['event_id', 'event_name', 'event_date', 'type', 'notes', 'location', 'status'], 'major': ['major_id', 'major_name', 'department', 'college'], 'zip_code': ['zip_code', 'type', 'city', 'county', 'state', 'short_state'], 'attendance': ['link_to_event', 'link_to_member'], 'budget': ['budget_id', 'category', 'spent', 'remaining', 'amount', 'event_status', 'link_to_event'], 'expense': ['expense_id', 'expense_description', 'expense_date', 'cost', 'approved', 'link_to_member', 'link_to_budget'], 'income': ['income_id', 'date_received', 'amount', 'source', 'notes', 'link_to_member'], 'member': ['member_id', 'first_name', 'last_name', 'email', 'position', 't_shirt_size', 'phone', 'zip', 'link_to_major']}"}]
solution: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T2.amount = 50 THEN 1.0 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T2.income_id) FROM member AS T1 INNER JOIN income AS T2 ON T1.member_id = T2.link_to_member WHERE T1.position = 'Member' AND T1.t_shirt_size = 'Medium'
completion:
```sql
SELECT 
    CAST(SUM(IIF(E.link_to_member = 'member_id', 1, 0)) AS FLOAT) * 100 / COUNT(DISTINCT E.link_to_member) AS Percentage
FROM 
    expense E
JOIN 
    income I ON E.link_to_member = I.link_to_member
WHERE 
    E.link_to_member IN 
    (
        SELECT M.member_id 
        FROM 
            member M
        WHERE 
            M.t_shirt_size = 'medium'
    )
AND 
    I.amount = 50
AND 
    E.amount = 50;
```

Please provide links to events for members who have paid more than 50 dollar.?
### Schema
{'event': ['event_id', 'event_name', 'event_date', 'type', 'notes', 'location', 'status'], 'major': ['major_id', 'major_name', 'department', 'college'], 'zip_code': ['zip_code', 'type', 'city', 'county', 'state', 'short_state'], 'attendance': ['link_to_event', 'link_to_member'], 'budget': ['budget_id', 'category', 'spent', 'remaining', 'amount', 'event_status', 'link_to_event'], 'expense': ['expense_id', 'expense_description', 'expense_date', 'cost', 'approved', 'link_to_member', 'link_to_budget'], 'income': ['income_id', 'date_received', 'amount', 'source', 'notes', 'link_to_member'], 'member': ['member_id', 'first_name', 'last_name', 'email', 'position', 't_shirt_size', 'phone', 'zip', 'link_to_major']}"}]
solution: SELECT DISTINCT T3.link_to_event FROM expense AS T1 INNER JOIN member AS T2 ON T1.link_to_member = T2.member_id INNER JOIN attendance AS T3 ON T2.member_id = T3.link_to_member WHERE T1.cost > 50
completion:
```sql
SELECT e.link_to_event 
FROM attendance a
JOIN expense e ON a.link_to_member = e.link_to_member
WHERE e.link_to_member IN (
    SELECT m.link_to_member 
    FROM member m
    WHERE m.zip = (
        SELECT z.zip_code 
        FROM zip_code z
        WHERE z.link_to_event = e.link_to_event
        GROUP BY z.zip_code
        HAVING SUM(i.amount) > 50
    )
)
AND e.link_to_member IN (
    SELECT m.link_to_member 
    FROM member m
    WHERE m.zip = (
        SELECT z.zip_code 
        FROM zip_code z
        WHERE z.link_to_event = e.link_to_event
        GROUP BY z.zip_code
        HAVING SUM(i.amount) > 50
    )
)
AND e.link_to_event IN (
    SELECT b.link_to_event 
    FROM budget b
    JOIN expense e ON b.link_to_event = e.link_to_event
    WHERE e.link_to_member IN (
        SELECT m.link_to_member 
        FROM member m
        WHERE m.zip = (
            SELECT z.zip_code 
            FROM zip_code z
            WHERE z.link_to_event = e.link_to_event
            GROUP BY z.zip_code
            HAVING SUM(i.amount) > 50
        )
    )
)
```

Note: The provided solution assumes the presence of a member's zip code in the `zip_code` table and that the `zip_code` is used to aggregate the sum of incomes for each event. The query is complex due to the nested subqueries and the need to validate the condition for multiple tables.

solution: SELECT T1.event_name FROM event AS T1 INNER JOIN budget AS T2 ON T1.event_id = T2.link_to_event INNER JOIN expense AS T3 ON T2.budget_id = T3.link_to_budget WHERE T2.category = 'Parking' AND T3.cost < (SELECT AVG(cost) FROM expense)
completion:
```sql
SELECT e.event_name
FROM event e
JOIN (
    SELECT bs.event_id, AVG(bs.cost) as avg_cost
    FROM budget bs
    JOIN expense es ON bs.event_id = es.link_to_event
    GROUP BY bs.event_id
) avg_parking
ON e.event_id = avg_parking.event_id
WHERE e.event_id IN (
    SELECT b.event_id
    FROM budget b
    JOIN expense es ON b.event_id = es.link_to_event
    GROUP BY b.event_id
    HAVING AVG(es.cost) > 0
)

    SELECT b.event_id
    FROM budget b
    JOIN expense es ON b.event_id = es.link_to_event
    GROUP BY b.event_id
    HAVING AVG(es.cost) > 0
)
AND avg_parking.avg_cost > (SELECT AVG(es.cost) FROM expense es GROUP BY es.event_id)
```

What is the event that has the highest attendance of the students from the Student_Club?
### Schema
{'event': ['event_id', 'event_name', 'event_date', 'type', 'notes', 'location', 'status'], 'major': ['major_id', 'major_name', 'department', 'college'], 'zip_code': ['zip_code', 'type', 'city', 'county', 'state', 'short_state'], 'attendance': ['link_to_event', 'link_to_member'], 'budget': ['budget_id', 'category', 'spent', 'remaining', 'amount', 'event_status', 'link_to_event'], 'expense': ['expense_id', 'expense_description', 'expense_date', 'cost', 'approved', 'link_to_member', 'link_to_budget'], 'income': ['income_id', 'date_received', 'amount', 'source', 'notes', 'link_to_member'], 'member': ['member_id', 'first_name', 'last_name', 'email', 'position', 't_shirt_size', 'phone', 'zip', 'link_to_major']}"}]
solution: SELECT T1.event_name FROM event AS T1 INNER JOIN attendance AS T2 ON T1.event_id = T2.link_to_event GROUP BY T1.event_name HAVING COUNT(T2.link_to_event) = (SELECT MAX(attendance_count) FROM (SELECT COUNT(link_to_event) AS attendance_count FROM attendance GROUP BY link_to_event) AS subquery)
completion:
```sql
SELECT e.event_name
FROM event e
JOIN (
    SELECT a.link_to_event, MAX(a.attendance) as max_attendance
    FROM attendance a
    JOIN (
        SELECT m.link_to_member, COUNT(m.link_to_event) as total_attendance
        FROM attendance a
        JOIN member m ON a.link_to_member = m.member_id
        JOIN major mjr ON m.link_to_major = mjr.major_id
        JOIN zip_code zc ON m.zip = zc.zip_code
        WHERE mjr.major_name = 'Student_Club' AND zc.zip_code IN (
            SELECT zip_code FROM zip_code WHERE city = 'Ithaca' AND state = 'New York'
        )
        GROUP BY m.link_to_member
    ) t ON a.link_to_event = t.link_to_member
    GROUP BY a.link_to_event
    HAVING max_attendance = (
        SELECT MAX(total_attendance) FROM (
            SELECT COUNT(m.link_to_event) as total_attendance
            FROM attendance a
            JOIN member m ON a.link_to_member = m.member_id
            JOIN major mjr ON m.link_to_major = mjr.major_id
            JOIN zip_code zc ON m.zip = zc.zip_code
            WHERE mjr.major_name = 'Student_Club' AND zc.zip_code IN (
                SELECT zip_code FROM zip_code WHERE city = 'Ithaca' AND state = 'New York'
            )
            GROUP BY m.link_to_member
        ) t
    )
) t ON e.event_id = t.link_to_event
GROUP BY e.event_name;
```

Return ONLY the SQL query inside a ```sql ... ``` code block.'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': "### Question
What is the program id and the summary of the degree that has the most students enrolled?
### Schema
{'addresses': ['address_id', 'line_1', 'line_2', 'line_3', 'city', 'zip_postcode', 'state_province_county', 'country', 'other_address_details'], 'courses': ['course_id', 'course_name', 'course_description', 'other_details'], 'departments': ['department_id', 'department_name', 'department_description', 'other_details'], 'degree_programs': ['degree_program_id', 'department_id', 'degree_summary_name', 'degree_summary_description', 'other_details'], 'sections': ['section_id', 'course_id', 'section_name', 'section_description', 'other_details'], 'semesters': ['semester_id', 'semester_name', 'semester_description', 'other_details'], 'students': ['student_id', 'current_address_id', 'permanent_address_id', 'first_name', 'middle_name', 'last_name', 'cell_mobile_number', 'email_address', 'ssn', 'date_first_registered', 'date_left', 'other_student_details'], 'student_enrolment': ['student_enrolment_id', 'degree_program_id', 'semester_id', 'student_id', 'other_details'], 'student_enrolment_courses': ['student_course_id', 'course_id', 'student_enrolment_id'], 'transcripts': ['transcript_id', 'transcript_date', 'other_details'], 'transcript_contents': ['student_course_id', 'transcript_id']}"}]
solution: SELECT T1.degree_program_id ,  T1.degree_summary_name FROM Degree_Programs AS T1 JOIN Student_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.degree_program_id  =  T2.degree_program_id GROUP BY T1.degree_program_id ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1
completion:
```sql
SELECT dp.program_id, dp.degree_summary_name
FROM degree_programs dp
JOIN student_enrolment se ON dp.degree_program_id = se.degree_program_id
JOIN student_enrolment_courses sec ON se.student_enrolment_id = sec.student_enrolment_id
JOIN sections s ON sec.course_id = s.course_id
JOIN sections se2 ON dp.degree_program_id = se2.degree_program_id
WHERE dp.degree_program_id = se2.degree_program_id
GROUP BY dp.program_id, dp.degree_summary_name
ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
LIMIT 1;
```

### Schema
{'addresses': ['address_id', 'line_1', 'line_2', 'line_3', 'city', 'zip_postcode', 'state_province_county', 'country', 'other_address_details'], 'courses': ['course_id', 'course_name', 'course_description', 'other_details'], 'departments': ['department_id', 'department_name', 'department_description', 'other_details'], 'degree_programs': ['degree_program_id', 'department_id', 'degree_summary_name', 'degree_summary_description', 'other_details'], 'sections': ['section_id', 'course_id', 'section_name', 'section_description', 'other_details'], 'semesters': ['semester_id', 'semester_name', 'semester_description', 'other_details'], 'students': ['student_id', 'current_address_id', 'permanent_address_id', 'first_name', 'middle_name', 'last_name', 'cell_mobile_number', 'email_address', 'ssn', 'date_first_registered', 'date_left', 'other_student_details'], 'student_enrolment': ['student_enrolment_id', 'degree_program_id', 'semester_id', 'student_id', 'other_details'], 'student_enrolment_courses': ['student_course_id', 'course_id', 'student_enrolment_id'], 'transcripts': ['transcript_id', 'transcript_date', 'other_details'], 'transcript_contents': ['student_course_id', 'transcript_id']}"}]
solution: SELECT T1.student_id ,  T1.first_name ,  T1.middle_name ,  T1.last_name ,  count(*) ,  T1.student_id FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id  =  T2.student_id GROUP BY T1.student_id ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1
completion:
```sql
SELECT 
    s.student_id,
    s.first_name,
    s.middle_name,
    s.last_name,
    COUNT(DISTINCT ec.student_enrolment_id) AS number_of_enrollments
FROM 
    students s
JOIN 
    student_enrolment ec ON s.student_id = ec.student_id
GROUP BY 
    s.student_id
ORDER BY 
    number_of_enrollments DESC
LIMIT 1;
```

solution: SELECT T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Addresses AS T2 ON T1.current_address_id  =  T2.address_id WHERE T2.state_province_county  =  'NorthCarolina' EXCEPT SELECT DISTINCT T3.last_name FROM Students AS T3 JOIN Student_Enrolment AS T4 ON T3.student_id  =  T4.student_id
completion:
```sql
SELECT s.last_name
FROM students s
WHERE s.current_address_id IN (
    SELECT a.address_id
    FROM addresses a
    WHERE a.state_province_county = 'North Carolina'
)
AND s.student_id NOT IN (
    SELECT DISTINCT sc.student_id
    FROM student_enrolment se
    JOIN student_enrolment_courses sc ON se.student_enrolment_id = sc.student_enrolment_id
    JOIN degree_programs dp ON se.degree_program_id = dp.degree_program_id
)
AND s.student_id NOT IN (
    SELECT student_id
    FROM transcripts t
    WHERE t.transcript_id IS NOT NULL
);
```

completion:

```sql
SELECT s.last_name
```
